holes.name: LeanEval.RepresentationTheory.symAction_range_eq_centralizer_glAction
holes.body:
/-- One direction of Schur–Weyl duality: the subalgebra generated by the `S_k` action equals
the centralizer of the subalgebra generated by the `GL(V)` action. The hypothesis
`Invertible (k! : R)` over a field is the Maschke condition for `R[S_k]`. -/
theorem symAction_range_eq_centralizer_glAction
    {R : Type*} [Field R]
    {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [FiniteDimensional R M]
    {k : ℕ} [Invertible (k.factorial : R)] :
    Algebra.adjoin R (Set.range (symAction R M k)) =
      Subalgebra.centralizer R (Set.range (glAction R M k)) := by
  sorry
